steps.title: Translate no real solutions into an inequality
steps.content:
The question asks for the largest positive integer $b$ that makes the quadratic have no real solutions. For a quadratic, no real solutions means the discriminant is negative:
$$b^2 - 4ac \lt 0$$
Here $a = -2$ and $c = -32$, so:
$$b^2 - 4(-2)(-32) \lt 0$$
$$b^2 - 256 \lt 0$$
$$b^2 \lt 256$$

steps.title: Find the largest positive integer $b$
steps.content:
$b^2 \lt 256$ means $|b| \lt 16$, so $-16 \lt b \lt 16$. The largest positive integer satisfying $b \lt 16$ is $b = 15$.

The answer is 15.
